title: Code review finding
description: Single finding produced by the Claude review pipeline. Used as the structured-output schema for `claude -p --json-schema schemas/finding.schema.json`.

file.description: Repo-relative path of the file the finding applies to (e.g., 'ml-automation-llm/agents/llm-evaluator.md').

line.description: 1-based line number where the finding starts. Use the line of the first affected statement, not the surrounding block.

end_line.description: Optional 1-based last line of the finding range. Null/absent for single-line findings.

severity.description: Severity tier per CLAUDE.md. Only these values are reportable; STYLE/MINOR are filtered out at prompt level and must never appear here.

category.description: Sub-classification within severity. 'other' permitted with a populated `category_detail`.

category_detail.description: Human-readable detail when category is 'other'. Required if category=='other', else null.

message.description: What the issue is, in one or two sentences. Concrete and actionable. Avoid hedging language ('might', 'could be').

suggested_fix.description: Optional concrete fix. Code snippet OK. Null if the fix is non-obvious or context-dependent.

code_snippet.description: Optional excerpt of the offending code (≤10 lines) for reviewer context.

confidence.description: Calibrated confidence in this finding being a true positive. Used for human-review routing; threshold per CLAUDE.md.

detected_pattern.description: The rule/criterion that triggered this finding. Used for false-positive analysis when developers dismiss findings (provenance per WS-4 PM3-24).

is_new.description: True if this finding is new since the last review pass on this PR; false if previously reported. Enables prior-findings dedup.

allOf.description: If category is 'other', category_detail must be a non-empty string.
